response.justification: Bogdan has a broad software background with genuine geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map, and some exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his C++ usage is limited to a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a thesis-level Sound Position Server project — neither demonstrates the production-grade systems C++ expected for a routing data pipeline. His primary commercial stack for the past decade is TypeScript/Node.js, and there is no Rust experience, no routing or navigation tile processing, and no pipeline latency optimization at the map-data level. The geospatial work is oriented toward NGO data collection and visualization, not routing graph construction or tile generation.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective (Geo-Data platform) and Green Map GIS application, including speaking at FOSS4G 2019
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed microservices at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Broad full-stack adaptability and demonstrated ability to pick up new languages and systems under production pressure
concerns: No modern production C++ or any Rust experience — the role's primary technical requirement is unmet
Zero experience with routing pipelines, navigation tile generation, map data transformation, or traffic signal integration
Geospatial work is limited to NGO data collection/visualization tooling, not the performance-critical routing infrastructure this role demands
